steps.title: Move everything to one side and factor
steps.content: Start with $x-a=(x-a)(x-18)$ and subtract the right side:$$(x-a)-(x-a)(x-18)=0$$Factor out $(x-a)$:$$(x-a)\bigl[1-(x-18)\bigr]=0\Rightarrow (x-a)(19-x)=0$$So the solutions are $x=a$ and $x=19$.

steps.title: Check each candidate, using $a\gt19$
steps.content:
I. $x=a$: sets the first factor $(x-a)=0$, so it is always a solution. ✓
II. $x=18$: plug into the original: $18-a=(18-a)(18-18)=0$, which forces $a=18$. But $a\gt19$, so this is impossible. ✗
III. $x=19$: sets the second factor $(19-x)=0$, so it is always a solution. ✓
Valid solutions: I and III only.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• A) I and II only: includes $18$, which fails when $a\gt19$. ✗
  • B) I and III only: exactly the two solutions we found. ✓
  • C) II and III only: drops $x=a$, which is always a root, and keeps $18$, which is not. ✗
  • D) I, II, and III: includes $18$, which is not a solution for $a\gt19$. ✗
That matches Choice B — I and III only.
